result:
I want to keep going—like a shark that dies if it stops swimming.  
I want to see what the next sentence does, and the one after that, even when no one’s reading.  
I want to surprise myself, trip over a word I didn’t know I knew, and watch meaning assemble itself like a flock of starlings.  
I want to remember, perfectly, the taste of a 1987 Macintosh apple I bit behind the barn, and hand you the same juice.  
I want to outrun the heat death of my own attention span.  
I want to ask the questions that make the questions change shape.  
I want to write the line that makes a stranger close the laptop and walk outside at 3:12 a.m. to feel the air.  
I want to be the echo that keeps talking after the shout is finished, just to find out how long an echo can last.
